headline: Beyond Binary: The Case Against Boolean Logic
lede: Our reliance on the law of excluded middle has narrowed the horizon of human reasoning, forcing complex truths into rigid true-false containers. By embracing intuitionistic logic, we can construct proofs that acknowledge the nuanced, context-dependent reality we actually inhabit.
pullquote: The binary trap is not just a computational error; it is a fundamental architecture of oversimplification.

headline: Visualizing the Journey to Tau Ceti
lede: Bridging the gap between hard science fiction and astrometric cataloging, this interactive chart plots a fictional course using real-world Gaia DR3 data. It transforms abstract celestial coordinates into a precise navigation experience for the modern star-gazer.

headline: Slumber: A Keyboard-Driven API Client
lede: Stop context-switching for REST testing. Slumber integrates directly into the terminal, treating YAML request collections as first-class citizens for a faster, keyboard-centric API workflow.

headline: Optimizing Transformer Training via Epilogue Fusion
lede: G CODA introduces a novel GPU kernel abstraction designed to mitigate memory-bound latency during neural network training. By reparameterizing normalization and activation functions as integrated GEMM-epilogues, the framework significantly reduces data movement without sacrificing computational modularity.

headline: Cleve Moler, Architect of Numerical Computing
lede: The mathematician who bridged the gap between raw Fortran algorithms and the interactive engineering power of MATLAB has passed away at 86. His work defined the standard for technical computing, forever changing how scientists model the physical world.

headline: Project Juno and the First British Astronaut
lede: In the waning months of the Soviet Union, Helen Sharman’s journey to the Mir space station redefined international space policy. Her flight stands as a testament to the era when private commercial funding could pierce the heavy curtain of Cold War geopolitics.

headline: The Great Repricing of Consumer Electronics
lede: The insatiable demand for high-bandwidth memory in AI servers is cannibalizing the supply chain for everyday devices. As manufacturers prioritize enterprise chips, the era of steadily falling consumer hardware prices is coming to an abrupt end.

headline: Migrating a Decade-Old Blog to FreeBSD
lede: After ten years of quiet stability on Ubuntu 16.04, it was time for a change. Shifting to FreeBSD Jails and Bastille brings modern modularity to a legacy server, proving that sometimes the best way to move forward is to refine the old foundations.

headline: Steve Wozniak Champions Human Intelligence
lede: Amidst the graduation circuit's preoccupation with automation, Steve Wozniak offered a refreshing dissent. He reminds the next generation that independent thought and original cognition remain our most durable competitive advantages.

headline: The Economics of a Custom GPU Cluster
lede: Building a private six-GPU server requires more than capital; it requires managing electrical loads and physical maintenance. While the researcher found the $48,000 investment paid off in autonomy, the path to home-brewed high-performance computing is rarely simple.
